title: Echo reply uses local_addr instead of ip_header.dst for unicast
description:
File: rust/lichen-ipv6/src/lib.rs:760-761

When building echo reply:
```rust
let reply_icmp = echo.build_reply(local_addr, &ip_header.src, truncated_data)?;
let reply_ip = Ipv6Header::new(next_header::ICMPV6, *local_addr, ip_header.src);
```

The reply source is `local_addr`, not `ip_header.dst`. 

This matters for nodes with multiple addresses:
- If a ping arrives at a secondary address (e.g., ULA), the reply should come FROM that address
- Currently, it always comes from `local_addr` (presumably the link-local)

Per RFC 4443 Section 4.2: 'The source address of an Echo Reply...SHOULD be the same as the destination address of the Echo Request message.'

This could cause issues:
1. Reply from wrong address confuses sender
2. Checksum computed over wrong addresses (though local_addr is used consistently)
3. Firewall rules expecting reply from requested address may block

Fix: Use `ip_header.dst` as reply source instead of `local_addr`.
notes: Commit: ff67204af - Echo reply now correctly uses ip_header.dst as source IP (and for build_reply checksum). Comment updated with RFC reference and bead. Multi-address support fixed.
close_reason: Reply source fixed to use request dst per RFC 4443 (commit ff67204af). Multi-homed nodes now reply correctly.
